med-courgette-peach.title: Courgette and Peach, warm from a garlic refogado
med-courgette-peach.note: Quick, about 20 minutes. Peak courgette and a ripe peach arrive the same week, so let the peach stay cool and raw against the warm, sweet onions.
med-courgette-peach.ingredients: 2 courgettes, 1 ripe peach, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, pepper, bread to serve
med-courgette-peach.method: 1. Slice 2 courgettes into thick coins, salt them lightly, set aside. 2. Slice the onion and garlic thin. Soften them slowly in a generous pour of olive oil until golden and sweet, about 10 minutes, a refogado, low and patient. 3. Get a second pan very hot and char the courgette in a little oil until blistered and just tender. Leave it alone so it colours. 4. Off the heat, fold the courgette through the onions. Tear in the ripe peach. A splash of vinegar, flaky salt, a grind of pepper. 5. Tip onto a plate, thread more olive oil over, and eat warm with bread.
med-courgette-peach.grabOneMore: a bunch of basil or mint, in season, torn over at the end. It is what makes it sing.
med-courgette-peach.protein: grilled sardines, in season now, or a spoon of queijo fresco if you want it meatless

temp-leek-squash-borlotti.title: Leek, Squash and Borlotti Braise
temp-leek-squash-borlotti.note: A slow hour. The first cold week, when squash turns sweet and fresh beans want long, quiet heat.
temp-leek-squash-borlotti.stars: leek, winter squash, fresh borlotti beans
temp-leek-squash-borlotti.ingredients: 2 leeks, a wedge of winter squash, fresh borlotti beans, 2 cloves garlic, a bay leaf, olive oil, stock, salt, pepper, crusty bread
temp-leek-squash-borlotti.method: 1. Peel and cube a wedge of squash. Wash and slice 2 leeks thick, greens and all. 2. Soften the leeks and garlic gently in olive oil with the bay leaf until collapsing and sweet, about 12 minutes. 3. Add the squash and the podded beans, turn to coat, then pour in stock to just cover. 4. Simmer low with the lid ajar until the squash is tender and the beans are creamy and the liquid has thickened, about 35 to 40 minutes. Loosen with a splash more stock if it tightens too far. 5. Season, thread olive oil over, and serve in bowls with bread to mop.
temp-leek-squash-borlotti.grabOneMore: a handful of cavolo nero, in season, stirred in for the last ten minutes.

med-tomato-basil.title: Tomatoes on Toast, barely touched
med-tomato-basil.note: Quick, about 10 minutes. August tomatoes at their most generous, so do almost nothing and let them be loud.
med-tomato-basil.ingredients: 3 or 4 ripe tomatoes, 1 clove garlic, good bread, olive oil, flaky salt, basil
med-tomato-basil.method: 1. Toast or grill thick slices of bread. Rub each one with a cut garlic clove. 2. Halve the ripe tomatoes and grate the cut side down to the skin, catching all the pulp. Or chop them roughly if you would rather. 3. Spoon the tomato over the warm toast. A flood of olive oil and flaky salt. 4. Tear over basil and eat at once, while the bread is still crisp.
med-tomato-basil.grabOneMore: basil, in season, and it is the whole difference.
med-tomato-basil.protein: drape over anchovies or a soft-boiled egg; for meatless heft, a slice of burrata or queijo fresco
